OperationId `Health.check` (set in health_check_docs) + the `{"status":"ok"}` 200 body + the `health` tag are unchanged. GET /api/health still unauthenticated (no RequirePermissions). golden E8 confirms the route+schema is byte-identical.

schemars keys HealthResponse by short ident, so moving its defining crate leaves the OpenAPI schema name identical. Regenerated ui+desktop: types.ts BYTE-IDENTICAL, openapi.json canonically-equal (jq -S) vs .extraction/baseline. Generated paths reverted via git checkout.

health/mod.rs `pub use ziee_health::{handlers, routes, types};` re-exports routes so AppModule::register_routes' `routes()` call resolves in the value namespace (crate-root fn via the crate's `pub use routes::routes`). No E0252 (only one import of the fn). ziee + ziee-desktop compile exit 0.

ziee-health Cargo.toml deps (serde/serde_json/schemars/axum/aide) version-match the ziee server catalog → single src-app/Cargo.lock unifies (no duplicate build). server/Cargo.toml adds the path dep. sdk `cargo check --workspace` exit 0; ziee/ziee-desktop check exit 0.

ziee-health names NO app type + NO DB: types=serde/schemars; handlers=aide/axum/serde_json; routes=aide. Grep confirms no crate::modules, no ziee_core/identity/framework, no sqlx/query!. The ONLY app tie (AppModule/MODULE_ENTRIES registration) correctly stayed in ziee. Genuinely reusable + build-DB-free.

cargo test -p ziee-health => 5 passed (health_check_returns_ok_200, health_check_returns_200_and_ok_status, health_response_serde_round_trips_to_status_object, health_check_returns_ok_status, health_response_serializes_to_status_field). Moved verbatim with handlers.rs; no assertion edited.

No NEW warnings from the health move. No build.rs/build DB introduced (0-migration module). tokio is dev-only (the handler tests); no runtime tokio dep added to a crate that only needs sync serialization.
